{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01FkGGggLbaBZRFcsGWp1dNj","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"The user is asking about a pharmacogenomics recommendation for ivacaftor in a patient with CFTR genotype classified as \"ivacaftor non-responsive in CF patients.\"\n\nLet me think about this carefully:\n\n**Ivacaftor (Kalydeco):**\n- Ivacaftor is a CFTR potentiator - it works by potentiating (increasing the activity of) the CFTR protein channel\n- It is approved for patients with specific CFTR mutations that result in a CFTR protein that is present at the cell surface but has reduced channel opening (gating mutations)\n- The key approved mutations include G551D and other gating mutations (e.g., G178R, S549N, S549R, G551S, G1244E, S1251N, S1255P, G1349D)\n\n\n- It's also approved for certain residual function mutations\n\n**CFTR Non-responsive mutations for ivacaftor:**\n- The most common CF mutation is F508del, accounting for ~70% of CF alleles, which causes a protein folding and trafficking defect that prevents sufficient CFTR from reaching the cell surface\n- Ivacaftor monotherapy won't work for F508del homozygous patients since potentiating the channel is ineffective when there's inadequate protein at the surface\n- For F508del patients, combination therapies like lumacaftor/ivacaftor or tezacaftor/ivacaftor are needed to address both the trafficking defect and channel function\n- Class I mutations that prevent any CFTR protein production also don't respond to ivacaftor since there's nothing to potentiate\n\n**Clinical implication for \"CFTR ivacaftor non-responsive in CF patients\":**\n- This classification means the patient has CFTR mutations that won't respond to ivacaftor alone—either producing no protein or causing severe misfolding\n- Monotherapy with ivacaftor won't provide clinical benefit, so alternative treatment strategies should be considered instead Formatting the clinical recommendation as JSON...","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":3,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1184,"cache_read_input_tokens":18420,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1184,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":0,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"ae696c4a-ff82-488f-bb02-da8b5eb69271","uuid":"e4dee872-ca64-4e0d-9387-63635c75e4c4"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01FkGGggLbaBZRFcsGWp1dNj","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01H2E3rDY8cJvwBgJQvRUipq","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"cat > /app/answers.json <<'JSON'\n{\n  \"recommendation\": \"Ivacaftor monotherapy is not recommended. Patients with CFTR ivacaftor non-responsive genotypes (e.g., minimal function mutations such as F508del homozygous, nonsense/frameshift mutations causing absent protein production) are unlikely to derive clinical benefit from ivacaftor alone. Evaluate eligibility for combination CFTR modulator therapy (e.g., elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor if applicable to the specific mutation) or manage with standard supportive CF care.\",\n  \"classification\": \"Strong\",\n  \"implication\": \"CFTR variants in the ivacaftor non-responsive category do not produce sufficient functional CFTR protein at the epithelial cell surface for ivacaftor potentiation to be clinically effective. Ivacaftor is a CFTR channel potentiator that requires surface-expressed CFTR protein to open; mutations causing absent protein production (Class I: nonsense/frameshift) or severe protein misfolding/trafficking defects without correction (e.g., F508del without a corrector) leave little to no target protein available, rendering ivacaftor monotherapy ineffective.\"\n}\nJSON\npython3 -c 'import json; json.load(open(\"/app/answers.json\")); print(\"Valid JSON confirmed\")'","description":"Write pharmacogenomics recommendation to answers.json and validate"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":3,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1184,"cache_read_input_tokens":18420,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1184,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":0,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"ae696c4a-ff82-488f-bb02-da8b5eb69271","uuid":"6eb9782c-4ad9-440b-aae7-6481a3c5cf3f"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01S57WNFnkYn4jbFoyS3YU8F","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The file is written and confirmed valid. Here is a summary of the clinical reasoning:\n\n**Drug-Gene Summary: Ivacaftor / CFTR (Non-Responsive Genotype)**\n\n| Field | Value |\n|---|---|\n| **Classification** | Strong |\n| **Recommendation** | Ivacaftor monotherapy is **not recommended** |\n| **Alternative** | Evaluate for combination modulator therapy (e.g., elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor) if mutation-eligible |\n\n**Pharmacogenomic rationale:**\n\n- Ivacaftor is a **CFTR potentiator** — it works by increasing the open-channel probability of CFTR protein already present at the epithelial surface.\n- Ivacaftor non-responsive mutations include:\n  - **Class I mutations** (nonsense/frameshift) — no CFTR protein is produced at all\n  - **Class II trafficking mutations** (e.g., F508del homozygous) — protein is severely misfolded and degraded before reaching the cell surface\n- Without sufficient surface-expressed CFTR protein, there is no target for ivacaftor to potentiate, and monotherapy provides no meaningful clinical benefit.\n- This recommendation aligns with **CPIC guidelines** for CFTR modulator therapy, which classify this as a Strong recommendation against ivacaftor monotherapy in non-responsive genotypes."}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":438,"cache_read_input_tokens":21638,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":438,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"ae696c4a-ff82-488f-bb02-da8b5eb69271","uuid":"18e61294-fc78-4261-9d25-df3d7c9d3676"}
